node.text: Some great books I’ve read over the last few years. Drop some more in the comments I’m looking for my next 📖
a creative act was word vomit
@shawn_zappo I loved this interaction and glad no one was putting anyone else down. Made me smile. Have a good day y'all.
@kafkasuccesor 🙏🏻🧡🙏🏻
This book changed everything for me but to each their own I guess. Maybe another read will unlock some things for you, happened to me with the Power of Now
The artist way is also pretty good too
@ayodroop thank you droop I'll remember this, didn't j Cole read this too?
So guddd
Great recs
How is the artist’s way not on here. Thats the bible.
@moboopthanbetty 👏
Please make a post of book recommendations for artist recovery 🥺
The Mistral Daughter, a novel by Judith Krathz
The creative act is so amazing, learn a lot read ths
Is it ?
the war of art
creative act was peak

these r amazing bro, i especially love the creative act and the power of now, i definitely recommend reading "the alchemist" it's a great story about the power of following your dreams and trusting your soul
The Power Of Now changed my life
the artist way by julia cameron as well
Honorable mention The Artist Way
lol
The War of Art by Steven Pressfield
Everytime the Rick Rubin book is recommended an angel loses its wings

@ivoasanto can you explain me why? Give advices to people who are not artists, and explain that everybody of us should make art due to be happy because art is what life is about, and that a lot of people are already artists witouth knowing it, for real It’s literally an a act of kindness to me, like giving wings to angels that had Forget how to fly
@simonegianninii you're not rick rubin, you're you
The inner game of tennis!! Look it up very interesting book
nah keep it
I highly recommend “swimming in a pond in the rain” by George Saunders! Great for writers and anyone interested in storytelling or art of any kind!
Modigliani Scandal by Ken Follet
How To Be A Famous Artist And Still Paint by Joe Inis
i think the Virgil abloh books should be up there, although they aren't really traditional books
Slop lol
